summary.scholar: On May 2, 2011, U.S. Navy SEALs killed al-Qaeda leader Osama bin Laden during a raid on his compound in Abbottabad, Pakistan. Bin Laden had been the mastermind of the September 11, 2001 terrorist attacks. The nearly decade-long manhunt ended with Operation Neptune Spear, authorized by President Obama. The news prompted spontaneous celebrations across the United States.
summary.explorer: On May 2, 2011, American special forces found and stopped Osama bin Laden — the man who planned the 9/11 attacks. He had been hiding in a compound in Pakistan. President Obama authorized the secret mission. When the news came out, people across America celebrated. The nearly 10-year search was finally over.

significance.scholar: The killing of bin Laden brought a measure of closure to the September 11 attacks and demonstrated American persistence in pursuing those who threaten national security.
significance.explorer: Finding bin Laden after almost 10 years showed that America would never stop looking for the people who attacked it on 9/11.

sources.citation: Miller Center: U.S. Presidents and Key Events
